text: 🔄 Forwarded from China and Geopolitics with Danny Haiphong: 🚨 LIVESTREAM ALERT: Join Danny Haiphong LIVE today April 9th 2pm eastern 8pm CET w/ Scott Ritter & Larry Johnson! Scott Ritter and Larry Johnson discuss the disastrous first full 24 hours of the so called ceasefire, including Iran shutting down the Strait of Hormuz again amid Israeli ceasefire violations and Hezbollah continuing military operations. What does this next phase of the war mean for all sides? Where do we go from here? Watch the stream via the link below! LINK: https://www.youtube.com/live/sySgSUEnpO4?si=0eSOiiU43fHgc5b8

NATO's Rutte: The question is: what would happen if China were to move against Taiwan? Why not first call your ‘junior partner’ in all of this—Russia—and ask them to keep us occupied here? So most likely, this would not remain limited to one region.
NATO's Rutte: I do not think that Ukraine’s NATO membership can be resolved in the short term. I don’t think it will happen politically anytime soon—that is my fair assessment.
❔ 🏁 🇺🇸 Rutti Frutti answers the most important question: is Trump his daddy? 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
NATO's Rutte: A couple of countries are holding back on Ukraine’s entry into NATO, including Germany, Slovakia, Hungary, and the United States. I don’t think it is on the table right now.
🇺🇸 Looney Loomer says there's something brewing in the Pentagon. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Q: Do you still consider Trump ‘daddy’ after yesterday? Mark Rutte: Well, on the ‘daddy’ issue—this is really a language problem. In Dutch, you would say that the translation of ‘your father’ is ‘daddy.’ And I said, well, yes—sometimes a ‘daddy’ has to be angry. So I was not calling him my daddy. Of course, the word ‘daddy’ also has a different connotation. And now I have to live with it—is this the rest of my life… But I own it, and the president owns it as well. He made a movie... so funny, this is why I like him so much.

Status-6 (War & Military News) RT @John_ForemanCBE : The MoD has put the red dot in the wrong place 😂 Olenya Guba in close to Murmansk, not Archangel. https://twitter.com/John_ForemanCBE/status/2042200181848850797/photo/1 BREAKING: The Ministry of Defence has released satellite images of the GUGI base the UK believes Russia deployed a series of submarines to covertly spy on Britain’s undersea cable network. It has also released an image of the alleged spy ship the Yantar, alongside at one of those docks. This vessel is thought to have tried to pinpoint and threaten undersea cables in around the UK in recent months. - Tom Cotterill tweet

NATO's Rutte: I know there are those who pleaded to continue the negotiating track, but hey listen, we know what happened with North Korea. There can be a moment where you've negotiated so long that guess what, North Korea got its hands on the nuclear capability, and then it is too late.

NATO's Rutte: When the time came to provide logistical and other support the United States needed in Iran, some allies were a bit slow, to say the least. In fairness, they were also somewhat surprised. To maintain the element of surprise for the initial strikes, Trump chose not to inform allies in advance, and I understand that. But what I see across Europe today is that allies are now providing a massive amount of support—logistics, basing, and other measures.
⚡ 🇮🇱 Red alert sirens are sounding in the Confrontation Line region, Northern Israel. @wfwitness

NATO's Rutte: Europe is assuming a greater and fairer share of the task of providing for its conventional defense, and from that there will be no going back, nor should there be. This is a move from unhealthy codependence to a transatlantic alliance grounded in true partnership.
